target.name: Liquid AI
target.founder: Ramin Hasani, Mathias Lechner, Alexander Amini, Daniela Rus

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $2.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Liquid AI is a Series A AI & Software company founded by Ramin Hasani, Mathias Lechner, Alexander Amini, and Daniela Rus, currently valued at $2.35B post-money after raising $287M. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising 8 private firms and 1 public comparable (Palantir), with valuations spanning $250M to $32.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a forward valuation of $4.7B from the $2.4B base. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company cohort yields $2.0B, reflecting the broad dispersion in the AI & Software space—sample peers range from Field AI at $2.0B (Series A) to Safe Superintelligence at $32.0B (Series B). The peer median is notably below the current valuation, likely reflecting stage-specific and capital-availability variance.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.7B to $5.4B, with a median of $3.4B. The current $2.35B valuation sits in the lower-to-middle portion of this range. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set (18x spread between 25th and 75th percentile proxies) and stage-progression timing assumptions. The disconnect between peer-median ($2.0B) and stage-progression method ($4.7B) reflects material uncertainty around comparable growth trajectories in the frontier AI sector.
